records.ety: [OE. cuischun, quisshen, OF. coissin, cuissin, F. coussin, fr. (assumed) LL. culcitinum, dim. of L. culcita cushion, mattress, pillow. See Quilt, and cf. Counterpoint a coverlet.]

defs: A case or bag stuffed with some soft and elastic material, and used to sit or recline upon; a soft pillow or pad.
quotes: Two cushions stuffed with straw, the seat to raise.

defs: Anything resembling a cushion in properties or use
a pad on which gilders cut gold leaf
a mass of steam in the end of the cylinder of a steam engine to receive the impact of the piston
the elastic edge of a billiard table.
A riotous kind of dance, formerly common at weddings; -- called also cushion dance.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cushioned (-nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Cushioning.]

defs: To seat or place on, or as on a cushion.
quotes: Many who are cushioned on thrones would have remained in obscurity.

defs: To furnish with cushions; as, to cushion a chaise.
To conceal or cover up, as under a cushion.
